name: # ATOMIC FILE I/O - Read and write files by path
readBytes
notes: NEW - read file as raw bytes (extracted from binaryDecodeFileOrFail)

name: # ATOMIC CONSOLE - Write to handles and read from stdin
readStdin
notes: read all of stdin as string

name: # ATOMIC ENVIRONMENT/PROCESS - Look up env vars and CLI args
envGetArgs
notes: returns raw CLI args as List String

name: exitWith
notes: exit process with ExitCode - never returns

name: withCreateProcess
notes: spawn external process - returns (stdin Handle + ProcessHandle)

name: waitForProcess
notes: blocks until process exits - returns ExitCode

name: readMVar
notes: blocks until MVar full - returns value without removing

name: takeMVar
notes: blocks until MVar full - returns value and empties MVar

name: putMVar
notes: blocks until MVar empty - puts value

name: # ATOMIC RUNTIME - Node.js specific and REPL state
putStateT
notes: persist REPL state to runtime storage

name: getStateT
notes: load REPL state from runtime storage

name: # COMPOUND FILE - Built from atomic file ops + pure functions
# COMPOUND CONSOLE - Built from hPutStr and getLine
replGetInputLine
notes: print(prompt) + readLine. Would be atomic if readline features (history/editing) needed

name: # COMPOUND ENVIRONMENT/PROCESS - Built from atomic env/process ops
# COMPOUND CONCURRENCY - Channels and fork built from MVar ops / Elm core
forkIO
notes: standard Elm Process.spawn - no custom runtime support

name: # COMPOUND NETWORK - Built from elm/http + pure functions
getArchive
notes: elm/http GET + pure ZIP decompress + pure SHA1. Currently bundled as single Impure.task for performance

name: # NOOP - Haskell API stubs that need no implementation
getManager
notes: Task.succeed Manager - HTTP manager is a no-op in JS
